key_insight: Elastic energy reduction can drive abnormal grain growth in ultrafine-grained Ni, selectively favoring grains with in-plane <100> orientation under cyclic elastic loading.
engineering_relevance: This suggests grain stability in thin-film and microstructural components can degrade even without plasticity, affecting reliability under repeated service loads.
method: Phase-field simulations were used to model grain growth under elastic loading.
Micromechanical analysis identified how stress state and local grain geometry influence growth pathways.
The framework was extended to compare behavior across materials with different elastic anisotropy.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports simulation-based evidence that the observed growth kinetics and orientation selectivity are consistent with elastic-energy-driven AGG.
watch_item: The mechanism is demonstrated computationally, so validating predictions against broader experimental conditions and material systems remains important.
paper_title: Phase-field modeling of elastically driven abnormal grain growth
